title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [freee-client.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L168) retries the whole MCP call on `429`, `5xx`, and “no result”. That is unsafe for mutating tools like `freee_create_deal` / `freee_update_deal`, because a partial server-side success followed by a lost/parse-failed response will be replayed and can create duplicate accounting records. Restrict retries to idempotent reads or add an idempotency/dedup layer. - Medium: [eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L389) now hard-whitelists many non-portable binaries (`claude`, `codex`, `cmux`, `systemctl`, `launchctl`, etc.) without checking `PATH`. That defeats the “phantom command” check on machines where those commands do not exist, so invalid instructions will now score as valid. - Medium: [agent-memory/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/agent-memory/experience.jsonl#L22) and [bookkeeping/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/experience.jsonl#L96) add raw session/task summaries containing operational details (`ssh mm`, host/workstation context, cloud region, account email). Committing these telemetry files leaks internal workflow data and creates unnecessary churn; they should be redacted or gitignored. - Medium: [context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh#L17) doubles the warning threshold and [softens the action](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh#L65) from an explicit handover to a passive `/compact` suggestion. If this hook is the safeguard against context exhaustion, this is a behavioral regression that makes missed handovers materially more likely.
